summary: Backend for 4 new messaging features works correctly (13/13 pytest passing). Frontend user-side (Flag button, report dialog, submit toast, typing signal) is fully wired and functional. HOWEVER: the Admin Reports panel and TURN config UI are BROKEN because the new /api/admin/messages/* and /api/admin/calls/* endpoints use JWT auth (current_user_dep) but the admin panel authenticates via X-Admin-Token header (ADMIN_SESSIONS). Result: every request from the admin UI returns 401 — 'No reports yet.' is shown even when reports exist, and 'Failed to save TURN config' toast appears on save.

ui_bugs.issue: Auth mismatch: adminApi(token) sends X-Admin-Token header, but /api/admin/messages/reports uses _admin_dep -> current_user_dep which only reads JWT from Authorization: Bearer or access_token cookie. Every call from admin UI returns 401. Reports never render (UI shows 'No reports yet.') and clicking Save TURN config shows 'Failed to save TURN config' toast. Confirmed via curl: X-Admin-Token=session-token -> 401 'Not authenticated'; Bearer session-token -> 401 'Invalid token'.

ui_bugs.component: Admin.jsx <select> with dynamic <option> children (line ~4809, data-testid=report-status-filter and possibly others)
ui_bugs.issue: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Dynamic <option> is wrapped by Emergent's x-ve-dynamic <span>, but native <option> only accepts text. Cosmetic hydration warning, does not break functionality.

integration_issues.issue: Admin cannot list reports, view thread, update status, or save TURN config via UI due to the X-Admin-Token vs JWT header mismatch above.

static_verification.typing_indicator: PASS — data-testid=typing-indicator at MessagesView.jsx:653 (three bouncing dots with animation-delay 0/150/300ms)
static_verification.call_debug_overlay: PASS — data-testid=call-debug-overlay at MessagesView.jsx:702 (font-mono bar with conn/ice/gather labels, only rendered when call.active)

runtime_verification.backend_pytest: 13/13 PASS (typing round-trip, expiry, auth-required, report create, self-report 400, admin list/thread/404, TURN override + fallback, ffmpeg webm->mp3 with audio/mpeg content-type)
runtime_verification.report_dialog_open_submit: PASS — dialog opens, testid=report-reason accepts input, submit closes dialog, 'Report submitted' toast rendered
runtime_verification.admin_reports_render: FAIL — 'No reports yet.' shown even though backend has reports. Console shows repeated 401 errors on /admin/messages/reports. Root cause: header mismatch documented above.
runtime_verification.admin_turn_save: FAIL — 'Failed to save TURN config' toast (visible bottom-right in screenshot). Same 401 header mismatch.
runtime_verification.admin_password_note: ADMIN_PASS in code defaults to 'Armin1234' (Balkin99 user) but backend/.env only has OWNER_PASSWORD='Dennis123.@@'. So /api/admin/login with Balkin/Dennis123.@@ returns 401. Only /admin/login-secret with 'haha123' works from UI.
runtime_verification.call_debug_overlay_e2e: NOT TESTABLE in headless (no mic) — static JSX check confirms wiring.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix _admin_dep in /app/backend/messaging.py so it also accepts the X-Admin-Token header used by ADMIN_SESSIONS (see server.py line 140 pattern: `if token in ADMIN_SESSIONS: ...`). Recommended: create a helper that first checks X-Admin-Token against ADMIN_SESSIONS (owner role synthetic user), and falls back to current_user_dep. Otherwise the whole Admin Reports tab and TURN config UI are non-functional — user cannot moderate reports or configure Twilio TURN even though the endpoints exist and work when called with a JWT.
MEDIUM: Fix the React hydration warning at Admin.jsx:4809 (dynamic value wrapper). Wrap the text child directly instead of via x-ve-dynamic span.

LOW: memory/test_credentials.md still says Balkin/Armin1234 but real owner password is Dennis123.@@ (per backend/.env OWNER_PASSWORD). Also mention that admin panel UI login requires ADMIN_USER/ADMIN_PASS env vars (default Balkin99/Armin1234) OR the /admin?haha123 secret URL. Update the file to reflect this.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth model inconsistency: /api/admin/* endpoints in server.py use X-Admin-Token + ADMIN_SESSIONS; new endpoints in messaging.py (admin_msg_router, admin_calls_router) use JWT + current_user_dep. This split is the direct cause of the 401 in the admin UI. Consolidate to a single admin auth pattern.

messaging.py _admin_dep returns 403 for non-admin users but 401 for missing token — inconsistent with the rest of the app; the admin panel toast shows generic errors so it's hard to debug from the UI.
TURN save-toast fires success on any 2xx, but does not verify the response body; if endpoint returned 200 with an empty body it would still say 'saved'. Consider validating {ok: true}.

chat_reports collection has no unique index on (reporter_id, reported_id, thread_key) — a spammy user can create hundreds of duplicate reports on the same thread. Add a rate limit or a unique/compound index with 'status=open'.
test_credentials: testbugfix1/password1 (regular user). Owner JWT: /api/auth/login as Balkin/Dennis123.@@. Admin panel: /admin?haha123 (secret URL) — the username/password form does NOT work with Dennis123.@@ because ADMIN_PASS env is unset and defaults to Armin1234.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 chat reports (one via backend test, one via UI submit) both from testbugfix1 -> Balkin with reason 'harassment test iter9'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin panel session tokens are stored in server.py ADMIN_SESSIONS (in-memory set), NOT JWT. New endpoints in messaging.py use JWT current_user_dep — mismatched. Login route /client/login uses math captcha (GET /api/auth/captcha, parse 'What is A op B?', POST identifier+password+captcha_id+captcha_answer). Owner password is Dennis123.@@ (OWNER_PASSWORD in backend/.env). Admin secret URL: /admin?haha123. Messages tab is inside /client/dashboard (data-testid=nav-messages), NOT a top-level route. Search dropdown result is a button:has-text('<username>'), not a data-testid.
rca of the issue: Root cause of admin UI failures: /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js exports adminApi(token) that sets header 'X-Admin-Token'. /app/backend/messaging.py's _admin_dep uses current_user_dep from auth_and_chat.py which only reads Authorization: Bearer <jwt> or access_token cookie. ADMIN_SESSIONS tokens are opaque random strings (secrets.token_urlsafe), not JWTs, so decode_token throws 'Invalid token'. Fix path A (recommended, small): wrap _admin_dep in messaging.py to first check request.headers.get('X-Admin-Token') against ADMIN_SESSIONS and, if present, synthesize a CurrentUser with role='owner' looked up by ADMIN_USER. Fix path B: have the admin panel call /api/auth/login for the owner in the background and store the JWT alongside bs_admin_token.
